title: haplopappus pinea
intro: Haplopappus pinea, also known as the sticky geranium or sticky hop, is a flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Haplopappus pinea include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
